evals.prompt: The Metabase dashboard query that shows average quality scores by niche over the last 7 days is taking 12 seconds to load. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Diagnose missing index on quality_scores(niche, created_at), prescribe CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY, and provide the optimized SQL query.
expectations: The response identifies a missing composite index on quality_scores(niche, created_at) as the primary cause
The response uses CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to avoid locking the production table
The response includes a WHERE clause filtering created_at to the last 7 days
The response checks for N+1 patterns (per-niche queries vs single GROUP BY)

evals.prompt: We need to add a view_count column to the agent_outputs table which already has 500K rows in production. How do we do this safely?
evals.expected_output: Provide zero-downtime migration: ALTER TABLE with DEFAULT, CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY, and a reversible DOWN migration.
expectations: The response uses ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN ... DEFAULT 0 syntax
The response uses CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY for any index on the new column
The response explicitly warns never to run CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY on production
The response includes the DOWN migration (DROP COLUMN)

evals.prompt: Our Next.js dashboard is making a database call for every video in the list to get its quality score. The page loads 50 videos and we are seeing 51 queries per page load. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Identify N+1, provide a single JOIN query, quantify 51 to 1 improvement, and mention Supabase connection pooling.
expectations: The response identifies this as the N+1 query pattern
The response provides a single SQL query using LEFT JOIN to fetch all videos and scores
The response quantifies the improvement: from 51 queries to 1
The response mentions Supabase transaction pooler (port 6543) for serverless Next.js
